title: ICU end-of-life care documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting that advance care planning was offered or completed, who was involved with consent, the person’s stated preferences, and where the plan is available, with capacity and legal context kept clinician-led.
assessment.rationale: Supports documented advance-care planning, patient involvement and involvement of important people with agreement.

source.title: End of life care for adults: service delivery — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE legal, capacity, consent, and end-of-life adaptation
source.population: Adults approaching the end of life and their carers or other important people, with consent and capacity qualifications.
source.setting: Coordinated health and social care, including advance care planning and transitions.
source.exclusions: Exact only when the clinician has established that the adult is approaching end of life. Supports recording advance-care-planning participation, preferences, carers, plan availability, and review at transitions; it does not infer prognosis, capacity, goals, or treatment decisions.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.7 — advance care planning and involvement

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting that advance care planning was offered or completed, who was involved with consent, the person’s stated preferences, and where the plan is available, with capacity and legal context kept clinician-led.

assessment.text: Supports documenting clinician-completed review of changing health and social needs, preferences, and advance plans at transitions without generating a care decision.
assessment.rationale: Supports review of needs, preferences and advance plans when health or treatment goals change.
